channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Here's when 2025 Election Day early in-person voting comes to a close across the US
item.description: Early voting deadlines approach fast across America with major races in New Jersey, Virginia and New York City. Find your state's cutoff date before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump team urges Oregon judge to end restraining order blocking National Guard
item.description: Judge Karin Immergut said she will decide by Monday whether to dissolve a restraining order blocking President Donald Trump from deploying National Guard troops in Portland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas’ congressional delegation wants Trump to punish Mexico for missing key water deadline
item.description: The state’s citrus industry is at risk, farmers say, after Mexico failed to deliver water it owes Texas as part of a 1944 treaty.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, Berenice Garcia, KPRC 2

item.title: Schumer accuses Trump of 'skipping town' during shutdown as president heads to Asia for trade talks
item.description: Sen. Chuck Schumer blasts President Donald Trump for leaving the United States for Asia during the government shutdown, accusing the president of "skipping town.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Poway Councilmember Tony Blain charged with four felonies days before recall election
item.description: The San Diego County District Attorney’s office has filed five charges, including four felonies, against embattled Poway City Councilmember Tony Blain.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Early voting begins in New York City mayor's race and contest to pick New Jersey's next governor
item.description: Polling places open Saturday for in-person voting in two of the year’s most closely watched elections: The New York City mayor’s race and the contest to pick New Jersey’s next governor.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DeSantis likely to sign bill allowing Ivermectin over the counter
item.description: In a social media post, Gov. Ron DeSantis said he’s pushing the legislature to pass a bill involving Ivermectin and he plans to sign it.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump heads to 3-country sprint in Asia, meeting with Xi as government shutdown drags on at home
item.description: President Donald Trump is heading to Asia for his first trip this term to the continent, where he’s expected to try to strike investment deals, work through trade talks and further some peace efforts.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration won't tap contingency fund to keep food aid flowing, memo says
item.description: The Trump administration says it won't use a roughly $5 billion contingency fund to keep food aid flowing in November amid the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Intel Democrats press Gabbard on Trump administration boat strikes
item.description: Leading Intelligence Committee Democrats are accusing the Trump administration of leaving them in the dark on strikes in the Caribbean that have killed more than 40 people, demanding information about the intelligence community’s role.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US sanctions Colombian president and family over drug trafficking allegations
item.description: Treasury Department accuses Colombian President Petro of enabling cocaine production surge while he calls sanctions a "complete paradox" in response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon accepts $130 million donation to help pay the military during the government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon confirmed Friday that it has accepted an anonymous $130 million gift to help pay members of the military during the government shutdown, raising ethical questions after President Donald Trump had announced that a friend had offered the gift to defray any shortfalls.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two candidates vie for DFL endorsement in race to become next Minnesota U.S. senator
item.description: Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan and U.S. Rep. Angie Craig joined Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition to talk about their ambitions to win the DFL Party endorsement in Minnesota’s 2026 U.S. Senate race.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he's ending trade talks with Canada over TV ads
item.description: The abrupt announcement comes after Trump accused Canada of using a "fake" Ronald Reagan ad to sway U.S. courts, raising the stakes in an already tense trade dispute.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: Hegseth’s order comes as the Trump administration has been striking alleged drug-smuggling boats in the Caribbean Sea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: New York Attorney General Letitia James pleads not guilty in mortgage fraud case pushed by Trump
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James accused the Trump administration of using the justice system as a “tool of revenge” after she pleaded not guilty Friday in a federal mortgage fraud case the president pressed the Justice Department to bring.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey early voting 2025: When locations open and how to find them
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) -- While Election Day is fast approaching, New Jersey residents will be able to cast their ballot as early as this weekend. In 2021, New Jersey established in-person early voting for registered voters at General Election polling locations.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, PIX 11

item.title: Politics Friday Special: Rochester's growth spurt is driving economic, political change
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst hosts a special broadcast of Politics Friday from Rochester, Minn., exploring how the city’s makeover plays into the regional hub’s economic and political standing.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Trump speaks at tele-rally to boost GOP's chances in governor's race in New Jersey
item.description: President Donald Trump is urging his supporters in New Jersey to vote for Republican gubernatorial candidate Jack Ciattarelli.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Social Security recipients get a 2.8% cost-of-living boost in 2026, average of $56 per month
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration's annual cost-of-living adjustment will go up by 2.8% in 2026, translating to an average increase of more than $56 for retirees every month, agency officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How presidents have changed the White House
item.description: President Donald Trump is demolishing the East Wing to make room for a ballroom. His administration says he’s continuing a presidential legacy of White House renovations, but this is the biggest in decades.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Transportation chief warns of travel impacts amid shutdown
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy has said the federal safety system depends on air traffic controllers working without pay.
item.dc:creator: Meg Hilling, NewsNation

item.title: Zohran Mamdani responds to ‘racist attacks’ from opponent
item.description: The comments are about much more than just his own identity, but a slight to all Muslim New Yorkers, Mamdani told NewsNation affiliate WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: What lawmakers are saying about Trump's demolition of the East Wing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — They're divided along party lines on policy. They're divided on the government shutdown. And now federal lawmakers are divided on the demolition of the East Wing of the White House to make room for the 90,000-square-foot ballroom that President Donald Trump wants to build.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rosen, Lombardo clash over health care, food funding amid shutdown
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Democratic Nevada Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo are pointing fingers at each other’s political parties as Lombardo pushes Rosen to vote with other Democrats in reopening the federal government.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump pauses planned San Francisco federal surge, gives mayor time
item.description: President Donald Trump wrote in a social media post that he is giving San Francisco's mayor time to "turn it around"
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Transportation Secretary Duffy ‘can’t guarantee’ on-time flights during shutdown
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy warned Thursday of possible flight delays and cancellations during the government shutdown, saying the federal safety system depends on air traffic controllers working without pay. “I can’t guarantee you that your flight’s going to be on time.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams endorses Andrew Cuomo
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams endorsed independent candidate former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo as his successor on Thursday. During Wednesday night’s final mayoral debate, Cuomo was the only candidate who said he’d accept an endorsement from Adams when asked by moderators.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: California leaders write letter to Hegseth, demand answers for artillery fire over I-5
item.description: CAMP PENDLETON, Calif. (FOX 5/KUSI) -- California Democrats sent a letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Wednesday regarding shrapnel that struck California Highway Patrol (CHP) vehicles during Vice President JD Vance’s visit to Camp Pendleton.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Wyoming Capitol evacuated after report of suspected explosive device
item.description: The Wyoming Capitol in Cheyenne was evacuated on Tuesday after a report about a suspected explosive device, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Florida bill seeks to label political offenses as hate crimes
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) - In the wake of recent politically motivated attacks across the nation, one state lawmaker is looking to change Florida statute. A new bill at the capitol is looking to expand Florida's hate-crime statute, shifting how hate crimes are defined and punished.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tribes struggle as shutdown continues
item.description: The United States is 20 days into the third-longest federal government shutdown in history and Indian Country is bracing for an unstable future ahead as reserve funding for many tribes could begin to dry up.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: DeSantis joins Democrat in nationwide push for term limits in Congress
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Governor Ron DeSantis is teaming up with a former Democratic representative to make a push for term limits in Congress. On Tuesday, the governor announced he and former Rep. David Trone of Maryland would co-chair U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge agrees to extend block on Guard deployment in Chicago while awaiting Supreme Court ruling
item.description: As the U.S. Supreme Court weighs whether to clear the way for the National Guard in Chicago, a federal judge on Wednesday agreed to extend a two-week block on Guard deployment in the Chicago area by 30 days.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Newsom deploys National Guard to support food banks amid federal shutdown
item.description: (FOX 5/KUSI) -- As the government shutdown reached its 22nd day, California Gov. Gavin Newsom announced on Wednesday he will deploy National Guard troops to support food banks throughout the state.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: What the federal government shutdown means countrywide
item.description: The federal government remains shut down. The NPR Network is following the ways the shutdown is affecting services across the country.
item.dc:creator: The NPR Network, MPR News

item.title: Senate Republicans go to White House in show of unity
item.description: As the government shutdown enters its fourth week, Senate Republicans are headed to the White House — not for urgent talks on how to end it, but for a display of unity with President Donald Trump as they refuse to negotiate on any Democratic demands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
